transcript: Hi! I need about six pans of Manchego—
—Okay, and I need that shredded, m’kay?
Um, so we do not shred cheese.
Oh, you don’t. Okay. I see.
No, we don’t. But we are able to get you the six pounds; we just don’t shred it.
Okay, I got a cookout comin’ tonight. Um—
Now, if I need about 14 lbs of Gouda, [pitch-up] is that something we could do, or…?
—I’m just gonna get an order form.
Now, what is the—what is the—what country of origin is Gouda, even? [sfx]
Um, ah, I see—well, which kind are you looking for?
A creamy, sweet flavor; a mild Gouda. [sfx]
I know aging plays a large part but, you know…
More of a subtle, base flavor.
Ooh! Nice! Okay, that sounds [delays] Oh, yeah—yeah—yeah—yeah—yeah!—

Um, yeah, and if you’ve got anything with sheep’s milk in it. You know, more of a grassy flavor, more of a tangier backbone to it, that would be something I would really enjoy for my cookout! [delays] Yeah—
Okay, so fourteen pounds red wax Gouda—
—Uh, you said you want six pounds of Manchego, right?
Yes, and, uh, is your cheese temperature-controlled up ‘ere? [sfx]
Okay, yeah. Can I—What else were you looking for? Um—
Anything crumbly, like, ah, maybe a—
—Did you want like a crumbly goat?
A crumbly goat, yeah. A [delays] crumbly go—ly go—ly go—ly. So, something like that, if you can get that. Like, if you’ve got like, ah, Taleggio—something like that, or a Reggiano, or—
Do you have Manchego at all—
Give me like one second; You just keep cutting out. Hold on—
—I’m fine; I can hear you fine!

You know what? It—the cheese always starts with milk; I understand that, but I’m trying to make a profound difference, here, with my cheese choices, am, that you’ve got in stock! Something less buttery, ah, than a Gouda.
Okay, so do you want something less buttery?
I would like Pecorino. Something found in a cave, ah, that’s real crumbly—sharp, real shārp! Sweet and kinda nutty—nutty flavor. You know, kinda [delays’] ’nutty—y—y—y—y—y’’
Kinda nutty. Something like that would be gr—
Ooh! Gruyère, yeah! [delays] Gruyère—Gruyère—Gruyère—Gru! That’s what I need—yeah!
Sir, were you looking for a platter?
Something that has a moist, crumbly, ah, sharp, sweet, nutty flavor, you know, from the yeast!
[speaking to employee] He wants moist, crumbly, sharp, sweet, nutty flavor.
Yeah. Something spreadable, too, I’m thinkin’, with a mushroomey kind of aroma.
[pitch-up] Ah, something mushroom—oom—oom—oom. Something mushroomey, I’m thinkin’, that you could put on a sandwich or bake into a crust. [sfx]
Okay, yeah. I’m just trying to figure out what cheese you want.
Something with a short aging period.
[speaking to employee] With a short aging period.

Something, maybe, you could use on a casserole or over some beans or something. Something that’s got, like a chalky finish in the [delays] mouth—uth—uth—uth—uth—uth—uth—uth. [pitch-up] Something real [delays] chalky—ky—ky—ky—ky! That would be real good! [sfx]
Sir, is there another phone that you could call us back on?
Well, I’m on a fax machine, here, my—somebody using my phone. [sfx]
Oh, yeah. We can’t understand you.
Do you have anything un-aged? [sfx] or anything brined?
Yeah, we have Feta. But, ah—
Can you crumble that for me? Completely crum—
Do you have something with a salty brine to it?
Yeah, the Feta, sir, but you’re—you’re cutting out. [road traffic sfx]
[pitch-up] Oh, I can hear you just fine up [delays] e—‘e—‘e—‘e—‘e!
I understand, but you literally sound like a remix!
You sound like you’re, like, [mimics delays] "uh—uh—uh—uh—ah—ah—ah—ah—ah!" [loop edit] uh—uh—uh—uh—ah—ah—ah—ah—ah! uh—uh—uh—uh—ah—ah—ah—ah—ah!
So everything you’re saying is doing that.
I can’t understand you, sir. I’m gonna have to hang up! Please call back from another phone!
[delays and fades] Something with a nutty flavor—nutty flavor—nutty flavor—nutty flavor!
